title: vicia alpestris subsp. hypoleuca
intro: Vicia alpestris subsp. hypoleuca, also known as alpine vetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of alpine vetch include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce stress and anxiety, and support the immune system. Alpine vetch is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
